subject: DevOps
title: Monitoring vs Observability
description: A micro-lesson that makes Monitoring vs Observability usable.

hook.text: The dashboard was green. Users were furious. The system monitored itself but couldn't explain why.

buildup.text: Monitoring asks: is the system up? Observability asks: why is it behaving this way?

discovery.text: Monitoring checks known failure modes. Observability helps you debug unknown ones.

twist.text: You can't write alerts for problems you haven't imagined. You need data to explore.

climax.text: Observability lets you ask new questions of your system without deploying new code.

quiz.question: What is the key difference between monitoring and observability?
options: Monitoring checks known failures; observability helps debug unknown ones
They are the same thing with different names
Monitoring is for servers; observability is for applications
